filter_pushdown.note: Column sorted ascending; zone map enables early break on trailing blocks only. Effective cardinality: ~58.8M rows.

group_by.key_encoding: composite (returnflag_byte, linestatus_byte) -> slot index via direct lookup table[3][2]

logical_plan.output_order: ORDER BY l_returnflag ASC, l_linestatus ASC — 6 rows max, trivial sort at end

data_structures.aggregation: flat_array[6] of AggSlot{sum_qty, sum_base_price, sum_disc_price, sum_charge, sum_disc, count} — indexed by (returnflag_idx * 2 + linestatus_idx)
data_structures.thread_local_aggregation: each of 64 threads maintains its own flat_array[6]; merged sequentially after parallel phase (6 groups × 64 threads = 384 additions total)

parallelism.morsel_size_rationale: L3 cache 44MB / 64 threads ≈ 687KB; ~7 bytes/row average for compressed cols → ~64K rows per morsel fits comfortably in per-thread L3 slice
parallelism.synchronization: thread_local_merge — each thread accumulates into its own flat_array[6], main thread merges after join (no atomics, no contention)
parallelism.note: No partitioning needed — 6-group aggregation has trivially small merge cost

index_usage.layout: [uint32_t num_blocks=600] then [int32_t min, int32_t max, uint32_t start_row] x 600; each block covers 100,000 rows
index_usage.purpose: early_termination — since l_shipdate is sorted ascending, scan zone map blocks in order and BREAK as soon as block_min > 10471; skips trailing ~2% of blocks (last few blocks near 1998-11-27). Gain is minimal at 98% selectivity but avoids reading final ~1.2M rows for free.
index_usage.usage_mode: mmap, read num_blocks, iterate blocks, break on block_min > 10471

physical_plan.scan_description: One pass over lineitem columns; filter (l_shipdate <= 10471) and all aggregations are fused into a single loop body. No intermediate materialization. Each row: decode compressed cols via LUT → check shipdate predicate → map (returnflag, linestatus) to slot index → accumulate into thread-local AggSlot.

operator_fusion.benefit: eliminates all intermediate row buffers; single pass over ~480MB of column data

physical_plan.memory_pattern_detail: All 7 columns scanned sequentially in column-major order. Byte-packed columns (quantity, discount, tax) have 4-8x smaller footprint vs double — reduces cache pressure and I/O volume significantly.

io_strategy.rationale: 98% selectivity means zone maps skip only trailing ~2% blocks, but cold-start I/O on HDD benefits from skipping even a few hundred KB. Primary benefit is the byte-packed columns reducing total I/O by ~3x on qty/discount/tax.
column_load_order: lineitem/indexes/shipdate_zonemap.bin (zone maps — tiny, enables early termination)
lineitem/l_quantity_lookup.bin + lineitem/l_discount_lookup.bin + lineitem/l_tax_lookup.bin (LUT sidecar files — 256×8 bytes each, ~2KB each)
lineitem/l_shipdate.bin (filter column — int32_t, 240MB; zone-map-guided: prefetch all qualifying block ranges)
lineitem/l_returnflag.bin + lineitem/l_linestatus.bin (group key columns — int8_t, 60MB each)
lineitem/l_quantity.bin + lineitem/l_discount.bin + lineitem/l_tax.bin (byte-packed payload — uint8_t, 60MB each; 4-8x less I/O vs raw double)
lineitem/l_extendedprice.bin (raw double payload — 480MB; largest single column)
io_strategy.parallel_prefetch_note: Issue madvise(MADV_WILLNEED) for all 7 column byte ranges concurrently using a prefetch thread pool before scan begins. HDD can pipeline multiple sequential read streams.
io_strategy.byte_packed_io_reduction: l_quantity, l_discount, l_tax stored as uint8_t → 1 byte/row vs 8 bytes for double → 7x I/O reduction per column; total compressed I/O savings: ~3 × (60M × 7 bytes) = ~1.26GB saved

estimated_cost.dominant_phase: main_scan (CPU + I/O bound in cold path; CPU-bound in hot path)
estimated_cost.hot_path_note: Hot: 58.8M rows / 64 threads = ~919K rows/thread; at ~1B simple ops/sec/thread → ~1ms compute, dominated by memory bandwidth. Target: ~52ms (matches GenDB benchmark).
estimated_cost.cold_path_note: Cold: ~900MB HDD I/O; parallel prefetch across 64 threads. Byte-packed columns reduce I/O by ~1.26GB. Target: ~442ms (matches GenDB benchmark).
